fact: December 10 is exactly 344 days into the year, which means there are only 21 days left until the calendar flips!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun sets so early on December 10 that some places see darkness before 4 PM - that's earlier than most kids get home from school!
Reindeer in the Arctic are growing their winter coats so thick by December 10 that each hair is hollow like a tiny straw, trapping warm air like a built-in sleeping bag!
December 10 falls during Sagittarius season, when this zodiac sign is supposed to be extra adventurous and love shooting arrows at targets.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, December 10 is prime summer weather - perfect for beach days while kids in Canada are building snowmen!
Tree sap in cold regions moves so slowly by December 10 that if you tapped a maple tree, you'd get maybe one tiny drop per hour instead of the gallons that flow in spring.
The moon on December 10 can look extra huge and orange when it rises because cold winter air acts like a magnifying glass, making it appear bigger than it really is!
Squirrels have been burying nuts for months, and by December 10 they've hidden so many acorns that they forget where 80% of them are buried - talk about being scatterbrained!
December 10 is only 11 days before the winter solstice in the north, which means daylight is shrinking by about 2 minutes every single day.
In places like Alaska, December 10 brings less than 4 hours of sunlight - imagine eating breakfast in the dark and having the sun set before dinner!
Penguins in Antarctica are in their peak summer breeding season around December 10, waddling around on ice while wearing their fancy tuxedos to impress potential mates.
December 10 marks the time when many evergreen trees like pine and spruce pump extra oils into their needles to prevent them from freezing solid like green popsicles.
